channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-18
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. In the **scienceverse/metacheck** project, issue #313 was addressed through updates to the DESCRIPTION file and module names in vignettes, showcasing the importance of collaborative issue resolution. Meanwhile, **giladfeldman/docpluck** made strides in documentation updates and rotation management, and **Davidvandijcke/coarse** introduced exciting internationalization features, including localized review headers and auto-detection of web languages. Notably, **JamieCummins/regcheck** underwent substantial enhancements, including bug fixes, code optimization, and the addition of new features such as HTML and OSF support, demonstrating the community's commitment to robust and user-friendly tools. These updates highlight the connections between our projects and the community's dedication to advancing open-source research tools.

107 commits across 6 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

regcheck35 commits
docpluck29 commits
metacheck27 commits
coarse14 commits
auto-zcurve1 commits

item.title: docpluck: 3 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, where documentation updates were made, specifically noting that "v2.4.91 shipped handoff + cycle journal" in the `docs` iteration. Additionally, on the `main` branch, a "canary" chore was performed, which involved onboarding "efendic/maier/xiao into rotating pool", with the rotation now being active. The `main` branch also saw another commit, although its details are incomplete, as it is cut off, but it is attributed to the same author, Gilad Feldman. No other branches were worked on during this time. The work appears to be related to documentation, rotation, and possibly other changes, all on the `main` branch.

3 commit(s) today:

  • f392957 [main] docs(iterate): v2.4.91 shipped handoff + cycle journal — Gilad Feldman
  • 7edc62f [main] chore(canary): onboard efendic/maier/xiao into rotating pool (rotation now active) — Gilad Feldman
  • 662c4df [main] @ — Gilad Feldman
  • cfe86f7 [main] feat: add optional extraction guardrails for size and timeout — Gilad Feldman
  • aa82a25 [main] docs(todo): queue investigation for items 3 and 8 — Gilad Feldman
  • 617db41 [main] refactor: surface fallback exception classes in method metadata — Gilad Feldman
  • 24aa793 [main] feat: add fallback telemetry counters and optional logging — Gilad Feldman
  • a5da768 [main] ci: add docs-code consistency guard — Gilad Feldman
  • f8594c3 [main] docs: sync normalization and benchmark claims with current code — Gilad Feldman
  • c8800bd [main] docs: add root README landing page — Gilad Feldman
  • 4c55d6e [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-16 resume handoff + concurrency-collision lesson — Gilad Feldman
  • 436fc5a [main] refactor(extract_columns): remove orphaned duplicate band re-extractor — Gilad Feldman
  • f7c3729 [main] docs(handoff): RC-1 Step 2 continuation handoff (post-deploy) + canary case-norm todo — Gilad Feldman
  • 818c3d0 [main] docs(iterate): cycle-2 learnings — RC-1 Step 2 + word-preservation-guard-is-the-safety — Gilad Feldman
  • da6c90f [main] docs(iterate): handoff — RC-1 Step 2 first cut shipped (v2.4.90, ship-dark) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1325d14 [main] feat(v2.4.90): RC-1 Step 2 -- per-band region-aware two-column re-extraction (ship-dark) — Gilad Feldman
  • 5782c6c [main] docs(iterate): correct handoff deploy-state row — main push HELD (local only) — Gilad Feldman
  • dca0e32 [main] docs(iterate): cycle-1 B7 (v2.4.89) learnings + lessons + resume handoff + TRIAGE — Gilad Feldman
  • 44f4ccd [main] fix(v2.4.89): B7 W0h -- recover dropped-minus coefficients from the layout channel — Gilad Feldman
  • 2cc32d9 [main] docs(skills): sync iterate spine to I1-I12 + extract parallelization ref; harden deploy preflight — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: coarse: 13 commits today
item.description:
Davidvandijcke/coarse

Work was done on the `dev` branch, as well as feature branches `feat/229-i18n-labels`, `feat/226-i18n-deenglish`, `feat/223-i18n-detect-web`, `feat/221-i18n-localized-review`, and `feat/219-i18n-schema-contract`. The `dev` branch saw the addition of GLM 5.2 to the model picker, as well as a fix to grey out unavailable Fable 5 and label Fusion in the model picker. The `feat(i18n)` commits added various internationalization features, including "localized review headers + completion email", "de-English-ify the analysis", "auto-detection + web language picker + RTL render", and "localized review output via --language". Additionally, a "multilingual schema + API contract" was added, and OpenRouter Fusion was added as a displayed model option with fixed dynamic-pricing estimates.

13 commit(s) today:

  • eae44c1 [dev] feat(i18n): localized review headers + completion email (PR-E) (#229) (#230) — David Van Dijcke
  • db35eb5 [feat/229-i18n-labels] feat(i18n): localized review headers + completion email (PR-E) (#229) — Davidvandijcke
  • ef7b339 [dev] feat(i18n): de-English-ify the analysis (PR-C) (#226) (#228) — David Van Dijcke
  • 3117681 [dev] feat(i18n): auto-detection + web language picker + RTL render (PR-B2) (#223) (#225) — David Van Dijcke
  • 8510764 [feat/226-i18n-deenglish] feat(i18n): de-English-ify the analysis (PR-C) (#226) — Davidvandijcke
  • a12f9dc [dev] feat(web): add GLM 5.2 to the model picker (#227) — David Van Dijcke

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 15 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the sole focus of the recent work, with multiple commits aimed at enhancing and stabilizing the repository. A bug related to "oss handling" was fixed, and "qwen handling" was hardened to increase robustness. The codebase was also optimized through the purging of "dead code" and an effort to "improve embedding efficiency", while also replacing a "deprecated llama" and updating the "llama model to the best available". Additionally, the repository saw the addition of new features, including "html, osf support", "claude functionality", and frontend updates such as "light mode", as well as fixes for "scroll and gpt-oss bugs". The repository also underwent "production hardening" and "prod preparation" in phases, and saw updates to its privacy policy with the update of "privacy.html".

15 commit(s) today:

  • e514303 [staging] fix oss handling bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 599489b [staging] harden qwen handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 8e7e452 [staging] improve embedding efficiency, purge dead code — Jamie Cummins
  • 88e039c [staging] replace deprecated llama — Jamie Cummins
  • 80dcf34 [staging] harden osf handling again — Jamie Cummins
  • d3471fe [staging] change llama model to best available — Jamie Cummins
  • 65d8ba6 [staging] production hardening - staging test — Jamie Cummins
  • 9c4cd96 [staging] prod preparation: phase 1 + 2 — Jamie Cummins
  • 57b6bc1 [staging] readd reasoning effort for oss — Jamie Cummins
  • 9735187 [staging] harden osf handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 8cddeb8 [staging] add html, osf support — Jamie Cummins
  • 978ec81 [staging] Update privacy.html — Jamie Cummins
  • 50f52d9 [staging] frontend updates - light mode! — Jamie Cummins
  • b4dc8e4 [staging] fix scroll and gpt-oss bugs — Jamie Cummins
  • 9a99761 [staging] add claude functionality — Jamie Cummins
  • 36a2d2b [staging] update pdf report handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 8b7ce6c [staging] update file viewer — Jamie Cummins
  • 9074b4b [staging] update model reqs — Jamie Cummins
  • 76b8a02 [staging] improve report viewing + qol features — Jamie Cummins
  • a90ee3f [staging] improve report UI flow + UX — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: 1 commit today
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, with two commits made to it. The commit on June 17, 2026, added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". On June 9, 2026, the `main` branch also received a commit that added "detectable statements and data repositories list", according to the commit message. The `main` branch is the only branch that was worked on during this period. The changes were specifically related to adding links and lists, with no other branches or modules modified. The work was confined to these two specific additions on the `main` branch.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 71c2a30 [main] Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF — giovannacdelsordo
  • aa35045 [main] Add detectable statements and data repositories list — giovannacdelsordo

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
